Game.Name: True Golf Classics - Waialae Country Club (USA)
Game.plot: Overview:True Golf Classics: Waialae Country Club ("New 3D Golf Simulation: Waialae no Kiseki" in Japan) is a golf simulator and an indirect follow-up to T&E Soft's earlier Harunaruka Augusta golf sim for the Super Famicom. As with that game, Waialae Country Club takes place in a prestigious US golf course (the eponymous Waialae) and features all 18 holes to play through.As with T&E Soft's other golf sims, more care is taken towards realism and simulation elements, meaning it is drier and more difficult than many other console golf games. It takes full advantage of the SNES's mode 7 capabilities to depict a 3D course.
Game.Notes: Grab your clubs and head for the first tee. This golf sim recreates the world famous Hawaiian Waialae Golf Course, noted for lots of bunkers and very fast greens. You can choose your golfer, assign him a caddy, pick your set of clubs and adjust the power, putting, technical, recovery and wind game stats to suit your personal preference. The game offers a practice training mode, a tournament mode and the ability to challenge your friends in a stroke match. Oddly, this game was not sanctioned by the PGA and assumes that the golfer is always male.
